title: medicago sativa subsp. caerulea
intro: Medicago sativa subsp. caerulea, also known as blue medic or blue lucerne, is a subspecies of the perennial legume Medicago sativa, commonly referred to as lucerne or alfalfa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Medicago sativa subsp. caerulea are attributed to its ability to support digestive health, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Medicago sativa subsp. caerulea include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
